headline: Volodymyr Zelensky: 'The pressure on Russia is insufficient. Not only from the US, but also from Europe'
description: In an interview with Le Monde, the Ukrainian president expressed concern about the consequences of the war in the Middle East on the conflict between Kyiv and Moscow. Pressured by both the Americans and the Russians to give up the Donbas, he defended the region's strategic importance and warned Europeans of the urgent need to lift the Hungarian veto on the €90 billion loan approved in 2025.

headline: France bids farewell to former prime minister Lionel Jospin in national ceremony
Trump says Tehran 'better get serious' on US-Iran talks as Israel launches strikes
Sarkozy minister's defense unsettled by memo in Libyan campaign funding appeal
description: A document pledging Libyan funding for Nicolas Sarkozy's 2007 presidential campaign caught former minister Brice Hortefeux off guard during the appeal hearing. The memo's authenticity has been greatly debated during the investigation and first trial.

headline: South Africa says disinvited from G7 summit after US pressured France
description: 'We are told that the Americans threatened to boycott the G7 if South Africa was invited,' reported the presidential spokesperson on Thursday.

headline: Night train service resumes between Paris and Berlin
description: The Belgian-Dutch cooperative European Sleeper is reviving the symbolic rail link between the French and German capitals that was discontinued by SNCF in December 2025.

headline: The sensuality of Henri Rousseau's paintings at the Musée de l'Orangerie
description: The exhibition 'Henri Rousseau: A Painter's Ambition' offers a stimulating reinterpretation of the artist's work, highlighting his rigor and discipline, far removed from the cliché of the 'naïve' or 'Sunday painter.'

Zinédine Zidane closes in on leading Les Bleus
description: Less than three months before the 2026 World Cup, the former French maestro is the clear frontrunner to succeed France's football squad head coach Didier Deschamps, who will step down at the end of the competition.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

headline: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
description: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.
